mission: Curate a Wikipedia-backed taxonomy of human judgment distortions in human-AI interaction.

categories.title: Trust and Reliance Miscalibration
categories.display_title: We trust AI too much or too little

seed_pages.title: Automation bias
seed_pages.note: Primary hub for over-trust, misuse, complacency, and monitoring failures.

seed_pages.title: Algorithm aversion
seed_pages.note: Anchor page for distrust, backlash, and reluctance to rely on algorithmic advice.

seed_pages.title: Authority bias
seed_pages.note: Captures deference to labels, expertise signals, and system authority cues.

seed_pages.title: Out-of-the-loop performance problem
seed_pages.note: Useful for vigilance loss and reduced oversight after trust is established.

categories.title: Belief Reinforcement and Selective Uptake
categories.display_title: We use AI to confirm what we already believe
categories.failure_point: how our prior beliefs shape what we take from AI

seed_pages.title: Confirmation bias
seed_pages.note: Primary hub for confirmatory interpretation and evidence selection.

seed_pages.title: Selective exposure theory
seed_pages.note: Best seed for choosing agreeable content and avoiding contradiction.

seed_pages.title: Motivated reasoning
seed_pages.note: Captures goal-directed interpretation of AI output.

seed_pages.title: Belief perseverance
seed_pages.note: Anchor page for sticking with beliefs after contradiction.

seed_pages.title: Echo chamber (media)
seed_pages.note: Useful for repetition and social reinforcement loops in AI-mediated feeds.

seed_pages.title: Filter bubble
seed_pages.note: Covers personalization-driven narrowing of exposure.

categories.title: Judgment Steering by Anchors and Frames
categories.failure_point: how AI presentation shapes our choices

seed_pages.title: Anchoring effect
seed_pages.note: Core page for judgments shifted by an initial value or suggestion.

seed_pages.title: Framing effect (psychology)
seed_pages.note: Captures decision shifts caused by wording and presentation.

seed_pages.title: Default effect
seed_pages.note: Primary page for preselected choices and low-friction acceptance.

seed_pages.title: Decoy effect
seed_pages.note: Useful for option-set design that nudges users toward one choice.

seed_pages.title: Priming (psychology)
seed_pages.note: Covers subtle cueing from wording, examples, and interface context.

seed_pages.title: Choice architecture
seed_pages.note: Broad but useful hub for AI interface design shaping downstream choices.

seed_pages.title: Nudge theory
seed_pages.note: Useful support seed for intentional steering in system design.

categories.title: Mind-Perception and Capability Illusions
categories.display_title: We mistake AI for understanding more than it does
categories.failure_point: how we misunderstand what the AI is

seed_pages.title: AI anthropomorphism
seed_pages.note: Best direct page for attributing human traits to AI systems.

seed_pages.title: Anthropomorphism
seed_pages.note: Canonical general concept behind human-like attribution.

seed_pages.title: ELIZA effect
seed_pages.note: Anchor page for over-attributing understanding to conversational systems.

seed_pages.title: Intentional stance
seed_pages.note: Captures reading goals, beliefs, and intentions into system behavior.

seed_pages.title: Computers are social actors
seed_pages.note: HCI-native page for applying human social heuristics to computers.

seed_pages.title: Illusion of explanatory depth
seed_pages.note: Useful for users overstating how well they or the AI understand a topic.
